text:
There was an old, wise owl who lived in a copse of ancient oak trees. Every morning, she would call to her pet raven friend, Old Wyrd.

They would chatter away all morning, reminiscing about ancient times. Old Wyrd loved recounting stories of legendary heroes from old tales. In springtime, he would weave dreams into flight plans.

Once, Old Wyrd spun a tale about a brave knight seeking the legendary Mirror of Reflection. This mirror had the power to turn anything it looked at into its truest self. Our hero journeyed deep into the forest to find it but discovered a clever sprite instead.

The sprite showed Old Wyrd the true essence of reflection—looking within, understanding oneself, not casting outward reflections.

The knight listened intently, understanding that the true power was not in seeing but in loving oneself fiercely.

Returning home, the knight shared this wisdom, teaching that the beauty of the world lay not within sights seen but in what lies reflective inside.
